spec_match.rationale: The page probes Proofreader, Rewriter, and Summarizer availability, then compares proofreading, rewriting, and summarising behavior with deterministic fallbacks when the built-in AI services are unavailable.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised Run all three, the tone selector, typo/clean/empty/long presets, and simulated API rejection; each control changed the visible outputs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature-page promise of comparing Proofreader with sibling on-device text APIs while showing that Proofreader preserves meaning and reports corrections.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now exposes unsupported and unavailable availability states, empty input, longer text, clean text, tone changes, and an explicit API rejection fallback branch.

design_system.rationale: The touched styles use shared CSS variables, pair button backgrounds with text colors, and avoid raw hex, inline styles, and unsafe HTML injection.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 at /v139/proofreader-api/proofreader-vs-rewriter/?verify=after-proofreader-comparison-v5. The repaired script loads, availability cards report Proofreader/Rewriter missing and Summarizer unavailable, Run all three produces six default fallback corrections plus rewrite/summary output, the tone selector changes Rewriter output, Empty input reports zero/no-input states, Long note uses scenario-specific correction/rewrite/summary output, and simulated API rejection labels fallback-after-error output. Concept route network requests were 200/204; the only concept-console warning was Chrome's expected built-in AI service-not-running warning. The feature conformance route /v139/proofreader-api/conformance/?verify=proofreader-comparison-patched reported 6 pass / 0 fail with no console messages.
